keyPoints: Provides a dedicated Specialized Operating Systems platform engineered to a minimal, highly secure, Chromium-based Linux operating system image pre-configured to run containerized workloads..
Integrates Container-Optimized OS capabilities directly into multi-tier cloud architectures, serving as a key component of the Application Hosting and Compute Infrastructure portfolio.
Encourages standard interface structures (REST APIs, Client SDKs, or console controls) to facilitate rapid cloud resource provisioning.
Runs application logic across a spectrum of compute environments, from virtual machines to serverless runtimes.
Provides horizontal and vertical autoscaling mechanisms to match computing capacity with incoming request spikes.
Enables zero-downtime maintenance and deployment updates through load-balanced instance groups or revisions.
Optimizes Container-Optimized OS performance through automated platform scaling and resource balancing.
Enables resource segregation, logical naming conventions, and tag-based configuration policies for Container-Optimized OS.
Integrates with VPC routing and private service access architectures to secure communication tunnels.
Governs access to Container-Optimized OS using standard IAM roles (e.g. roles/containeroptimizedos.admin and roles/containeroptimizedos.viewer) for fine-grained permissions.
Supports audit logging to record administrative actions and verify API configuration modifications for compliance reports.
Secures operational payloads using encryption at rest (Google-managed or Customer-Managed Keys via KMS) and SSL/TLS in transit.
Offers regional and multi-regional configuration options, duplicating service state across multiple zones to sustain outage events.
Enforces API quotas and rate limits programmatically to prevent resource starvation and noisy-neighbor interference.
Provides service recovery tools, regular status logs, and high-availability SLAs matching enterprise uptime goals.
Billed based on resource metrics (e.g., operations count, active connections, or gigabytes stored) allowing cost optimization.
Integrates with Cloud Monitoring and Cloud Logging to enable real-time alerting, metrics tracking, and diagnostic dashboards.
Enables command line administration through the gcloud CLI tool, allowing pipeline automation and scripting.

scenarios.scenario: An enterprise wants to construct a secure architectural pattern involving Container-Optimized OS that blocks public internet ingress and enforces strict access restrictions.
scenarios.solution: Deploy the Container-Optimized OS resource inside a VPC Service Control perimeter, routing connections privately using Serverless VPC connectors or internal gateways, and assign permissions using IAM roles.

scenarios.scenario: A development team needs to build automated CI/CD configurations that configure and audit Container-Optimized OS operations on a regular basis while tracking costs.
scenarios.solution: Use Cloud Build scripts executing 'gcloud' commands to provision the Container-Optimized OS resources, enable Cloud Audit Logs, and configure budget notifications at the project folder level.

commands.description: Create or configure a new Container-Optimized OS resource
commands.command:
gcloud ai create prod-containeroptimizedos-resource \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --description="Production Container-Optimized OS Resource"

commands.description: List active Container-Optimized OS instances and query configuration states
